title: ESCALATE: No interval lower bound validation in server config

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"No interval lower bound validation","severity":"Useful","description":"--interval CLI argument accepts 0 and negative values. Interval 0 causes continuous scraping in a tight loop, potentially hammering claude.ai. The old tmux-status-quota-poll had MIN_INTERVAL = 30 but the new server has no such guard. Confirmed by boundary value tests.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Validate in parse_args()","solution":"Add: if args.interval < 30: parser.error('--interval must be >= 30'). Matches old behavior.","pros":"Prevents accidental DoS, consistent with old MIN_INTERVAL = 30, clear error message","cons":"Minor code addition"},{"label":"Option 2: Clamp in QuotaServer.__init__","solution":"self.interval = max(30, interval)","pros":"Defense at usage site","cons":"Silently changes user input, which may surprise the operator"}],"recommendation":"Option 1 — explicit validation with clear error message","promotion_reason":"Natural FIX promoted to ESCALATE — max fix cycles (3) reached after 4 cycles"}
